tiers.name: Short Vowels & CVC Words
tiers.description: Master foundational consonant-vowel-consonant words with short vowel sounds.
tiers.rule: Listen for the short crisp vowel sound between two consonants.
tiers.lessonScript: Welcome, young Padawan! Your training starts with foundational CVC words. Just like stacking basic blocks in Minecraft to build a sturdy shelter before nightfall, you build these words one crisp, short vowel sound at a time. Master the short vowels, and the Force will be with you!

sections.name: Short A and I Words
sections.rule: Short 'a' sounds like the start of 'apple', and short 'i' sounds like the start of 'igloo'.
sections.lessonScript: In Minecraft, craft a 'MAP' to find a pink 'PIG', or watch Hermione's 'CAT' Crookshanks 'SIT' on a wizard's 'HAT'. Short 'a' sounds like the crunch of an apple, and short 'i' sounds like an igloo. Keep your vowel sounds short and crisp to 'WIN' the spelling battle!

words.definition: A small domesticated furry mammal.
words.sentence: Hermione's pet cat Crookshanks liked to chase spiders at Hogwarts.

words.definition: A visual representation of an area.
words.sentence: The dwarves used an ancient map to find the Lonely Mountain.

words.definition: A container made of flexible material.
words.sentence: Frodo carried the One Ring safely inside his small leather bag.

words.definition: A covering for the head.
words.sentence: The Sorting Hat placed Harry Potter into Gryffindor house.

words.definition: Feeling or showing sorrow.
words.sentence: Dobby was sad when he was treated poorly by the Malfoys.

words.definition: A domesticated hoofed mammal.
words.sentence: Steve fed a carrot to the pink pig in his Minecraft farm.

words.definition: Of considerable size or extent.
words.sentence: Hagrid is a very big and friendly giant who loves magical creatures.

words.definition: Be successful or victorious in a contest.
words.sentence: Harry managed to win the Quidditch match by catching the Golden Snitch.

words.definition: Drink by taking small mouthfuls.
words.sentence: Yoda took a warm sip of root tea in his swamp home on Dagobah.

words.definition: Rest with the body supported by the buttocks.
words.sentence: The hobbits decided to sit on the grassy hill and eat second breakfast.

sections.name: Short O, U, and E Words
sections.rule: Listen closely for the distinct short vowel sounds in the middle of these words.
sections.lessonScript: As the twin 'SUN's of Tatooine set, you must 'RUN' from Creepers and hop into a red 'BED'! Today we explore short O, U, and E. Listen for the distinct middle sounds in Hagrid's loyal 'DOG', a magical wooden 'BOX' from Narnia, or Darth Vader's 'RED' lightsaber. Mnemonic: shape your mouth like an 'O' for dog, and make a wide grin for red!

words.definition: A domesticated carnivorous mammal.
words.sentence: Fang was Hagrid's loyal dog who lived in the wooden hut outside Hogwarts.

words.definition: A container with a flat base and sides.
words.sentence: Lucy found a carved wooden box inside the magical wardrobe.

words.definition: The highest point or part of something.
words.sentence: Gandalf stood at the very top of the high tower of Orthanc.

words.definition: The star round which the earth orbits.
words.sentence: The twin suns of Tatooine shone brightly over Luke's desert home.

words.definition: Move at a speed faster than a walk.
words.sentence: You must run fast when a Creeper starts hissing in Minecraft!

words.definition: A small bowl-shaped drinking vessel.
words.sentence: The Triwizard Cup glowed with a mysterious blue magical light.

words.definition: A piece of furniture for sleep or rest.
words.sentence: Steve crafted a red bed in Minecraft so he could sleep through the night.

words.definition: A color at the end of the spectrum next to orange.
words.sentence: Darth Vader's lightsaber glowed with a bright red blade.

words.definition: The number equivalent to the product of five and two.
words.sentence: There were ten shiny Galleons resting inside Harry's velvet money bag.

words.definition: Each of the limbs on which a person or animal walks.
words.sentence: C-3PO had one silver leg that matched his shiny golden armor plating.

tiers.name: Silent E & Long Vowels
tiers.description: Learn how silent E at the end of a word makes the vowel say its own name.
tiers.rule: Silent E jumps over one consonant to give all its power to the vowel!
tiers.lessonScript: Behold the power of the silent Magic E! Like a secret Jedi Master hiding in the shadows, the 'E' sits silently at the end of a word, using the Force to jump backward over one consonant and make the middle vowel say its own long name! It transforms 'cap' into 'cape' and 'cub' into 'cube'.

sections.name: Long A and I with Silent E
sections.rule: The 'a_e' and 'i_e' patterns make long vowel sounds.
sections.lessonScript: Welcome to Hogwarts! Today we bake a giant birthday 'CAKE' for Harry and play a wizard chess 'GAME'. Notice how the silent 'E' at the end of 'TIME' and 'BIKE' magically forces the 'I' to shout its name! Mnemonic: When 'E' is at the gate, the vowel says its name!

words.definition: A sweet baked food made from dough or batter.
words.sentence: Hagrid baked a large chocolate birthday cake for Harry Potter.

words.definition: A form of play or sport.
words.sentence: Wizard's Chess is a famous magical game played at Hogwarts.

words.definition: A word by which a person or thing is known.
words.sentence: Most wizards were too afraid to speak Voldemort's actual name.

words.definition: A hinged barrier used to close an opening.
words.sentence: The heavy iron gate of Mordor was guarded by fierce Orcs.

words.definition: A ridge of water moving across the surface of a body of water.
words.sentence: A giant wave splashed against the wooden ship sailing to Narnia.

words.definition: A bicycle.
words.sentence: The children wished they had a bike to explore the magical forest.

words.definition: A toy tethered with a string and flown in the wind.
words.sentence: A colorful kite flew above the green hills of the peaceful Shire.

words.definition: The indefinite continued progress of existence.
words.sentence: Hermione used a Time-Turner to travel back in time for her classes.

words.definition: The number equivalent to the sum of two and three.
words.sentence: Five X-wing starfighters zoomed out of the rebel hangar bay.

words.definition: A long, narrow mark or band.
words.sentence: The Hogwarts students stood in a straight line for the Sorting Ceremony.

sections.name: Long O and U with Silent E
sections.rule: The 'o_e' and 'u_e' patterns make long vowel sounds.
sections.lessonScript: Step into Bilbo's cozy Hobbit 'HOME' or climb down an elven 'ROPE'! Today we master long O and U. The magic E turns a Minecraft skeleton's 'BONE' into a long 'O' sound, and helps Mr. Tumnus play a beautiful 'TUNE' on his 'FLUTE' in Narnia. Remember: silent E gives all its energy to the vowel!

words.definition: The place where one lives permanently.
words.sentence: Bilbo Baggins loved his cozy underground home in Bag End.

words.definition: A length of strong cord made by twisting together strands.
words.sentence: Samwise carried an elven rope that helped them climb down the cliff.

words.definition: A feeling of expectation and desire for a certain thing to happen.
words.sentence: A New Hope brought light back to the galaxy during dark times.

words.definition: A prickly bush or shrub that bears beautiful fragrant flowers.
words.sentence: A beautiful red rose grew in the courtyard of Hogwarts castle.

words.definition: Any of the pieces of hard whitish tissue making up the skeleton.
words.sentence: A Minecraft skeleton dropped a bone when the sun came up.

words.definition: One of a set of explicit or understood regulations.
words.sentence: The most important rule at Hogwarts is to never enter the Forbidden Forest.

words.definition: A long, hollow cylinder.
words.sentence: Luke floated inside a glass medical healing tube after his rescue.

words.definition: Attractive in a pretty or endearing way.
words.sentence: The baby Ewoks on Endor were very cute and loved to eat snacks.

words.definition: A melody, especially one that characterizes a certain piece of music.
words.sentence: The faun Tumnus played a magical tune on his flute in Narnia.

words.definition: A wind instrument made from a tube with holes.
words.sentence: Quirrell used a wooden flute to lull the three-headed dog to sleep.

tiers.name: Consonant Blends & Digraphs
tiers.description: Master two consonants that blend together or combine to make a single sound.
tiers.rule: Blends keep both sounds, while digraphs (sh, ch, th) make a brand new sound.
tiers.lessonScript: When two consonant heroes team up, they form powerful alliances! Blends keep both of their original sounds, like 'st' in 'star'. But Digraphs like 'sh', 'ch', and 'th' use magic to fuse together into a brand new sound, just like crafting iron and wood into a shield!

sections.name: Digraphs sh, ch, th
sections.rule: 'sh' sounds like 'shh', 'ch' sounds like 'choo choo', and 'th' is made with your tongue between your teeth.
sections.lessonScript: Enter Diagon Alley's wand 'SHOP' or sail across Narnia on a majestic 'SHIP'! Today we study Digraphs. 'SH' makes the quiet 'shh' sound, 'CH' makes the cheerful 'chat' sound, and 'TH' guides your 'PATH' through the Shire. Tip: place your tongue between your teeth when saying 'WITH' and 'THIS'!

words.definition: A building or room where goods are sold.
words.sentence: Ollivanders wand shop in Diagon Alley was filled with thousands of boxes.

words.definition: A large boat for transporting people or goods by sea.
words.sentence: The majestic Dawn Treader ship sailed across the eastern oceans of Narnia.

words.definition: A limbless cold-blooded vertebrate animal with gills and fins.
words.sentence: Gollum liked to catch fresh fish in the dark underground lake.

words.definition: Talk in a friendly and informal way.
words.sentence: Harry and Ron loved to chat by the warm fire in Gryffindor Tower.

words.definition: The protruding part of the face below the mouth.
words.sentence: Dumbledore had a very long silver beard that reached down from his chin.

words.definition: Having a great deal of money or assets.
words.sentence: The dwarves of Erebor were very rich from their vast mines of gold.

words.definition: Used to identify a specific person or thing close at hand.
words.sentence: "This is the way," said the Mandalorian as he put on his helmet.

words.definition: Used to identify a specific person or thing observed at a distance.
words.sentence: "That spaceship is too big to be a space station," Obi-Wan said.

words.definition: Accompanied by another person or thing.
words.sentence: May the Force be with you as you face the trials ahead.

words.definition: A way or track laid down for walking.
words.sentence: The Hobbits followed a hidden path through the dark woods of the Shire.

sections.name: Beginning Blends st, fl, tr
sections.rule: Blend the two consonant sounds together smoothly at the start of the word.
sections.lessonScript: Look up at the massive Death 'STAR' or climb a Whomping Willow 'TREE'! Today we master consonant blends. When you take a 'STEP' into Narnia or 'FLIP' Anakin's starfighter to dodge a 'TRAP', you hear both consonant sounds blended smoothly together. Listen closely so you don't 'TRIP'!

words.definition: A fixed luminous point in the night sky.
words.sentence: The Death Star was a giant space station capable of destroying planets.

words.definition: Come to an end or cease to operate.
words.sentence: Hermione told Ron to stop waving his wand around so carelessly.

words.definition: Put down one's foot in taking a stride.
words.sentence: Take a brave step through the wardrobe door to enter Narnia.

words.definition: A piece of cloth with a special design, used as a symbol.
words.sentence: The red Gryffindor flag fluttered proudly during the Quidditch match.

words.definition: Turn over or cause to turn over with a sudden quick movement.
words.sentence: Anakin could flip his starfighter upside down to dodge laser blasts.

words.definition: A woody perennial plant with a single trunk.
words.sentence: The Whomping Willow is a very angry tree planted at Hogwarts.

words.definition: A journey or excursion.
words.sentence: Frodo set out on a perilous trip to Mount Doom to destroy the Ring.

words.definition: A device or enclosure designed to catch and retain animals.
words.sentence: "It's a trap!" Admiral Ackbar shouted during the space battle.

words.definition: A tailless amphibian with short smooth skin and long hind legs.
words.sentence: Harry ate a magical Chocolate Frog on the train and found Dumbledore's card.

words.definition: A heavy stick with a thick end, used as a weapon.
words.sentence: The mountain troll swung a giant wooden club in the Hogwarts bathroom.

tiers.name: High-Frequency Sight Words
tiers.description: Practice essential words that appear frequently in reading and writing.
tiers.rule: Memorize these words by sight, as some do not follow normal phonetic rules.
tiers.lessonScript: These legendary words appear everywhere across the galaxy! Some refuse to follow the normal rules of phonics, acting like rebellious space smugglers. You must memorize them by sight and learn them by heart, just as a wizard memorizes their most trusted spells!

sections.name: Essential Sight Words Part 1
sections.rule: Practice reading and spelling these common sight words instantly.
sections.lessonScript: Welcome, Padawan! To read the ancient Jedi archives, you must instantly recognize sight words like 'THE', 'YOU', and 'THEY'. When Obi-Wan 'SAID' to use the Force, or when an owl arrives 'FROM' Hogwarts, these words keep the sentence flowing smoothly. Take a mental snapshot of each one!

words.definition: Used to point forward to a following qualifying clause.
words.sentence: The Millennium Falcon is the fastest junk pile in the entire galaxy.

words.definition: Used to connect words of the same part of speech.
words.sentence: Harry, Ron, and Hermione were the best of friends at Hogwarts.

words.definition: Used to refer to the person or people being addressed.
words.sentence: "You are a wizard, Harry," Hagrid said with a warm smile.

words.definition: First and third-person singular past of be.
words.sentence: It was a quiet and peaceful morning in the green hills of the Shire.

words.definition: In place of or in representation of.
words.sentence: Dobby would do anything for Harry Potter to keep him safe.

words.definition: Second person singular present and first, second, third person plural present of be.
words.sentence: The droids you are looking for left on a transport ship.

words.definition: Used to refer to two or more previously mentioned.
words.sentence: They flew on the backs of giant eagles to escape the fiery mountain.

words.definition: Past and past participle of say.
words.sentence: "Use the Force, Luke," whispered Obi-Wan's voice.

words.definition: Possess, own, or hold.
words.sentence: The Jedi Knights have glowing lightsabers to defend the galaxy.

words.definition: Indicating the point in space at which a journey, motion, or action starts.
words.sentence: The owl arrived from Hogwarts carrying an important letter.

sections.name: Essential Sight Words Part 2
sections.rule: Practice reading and spelling these common sight words instantly.
sections.lessonScript: 'WHAT' kind of magical creature is in Hagrid's pocket? 'WHEN' the sun rises in Minecraft, 'WHERE' do the skeletons go? Master tricky words like 'COME', 'SOME', and 'BEEN'. Mnemonic: 'COME' and 'SOME' look like 'home' but sound like 'hum'! Memorize them to unlock your spelling magic.

words.definition: Asking for information specifying something.
words.sentence: What kind of magical creature is hiding inside Hagrid's giant pocket?

words.definition: Second person singular past, plural past, and past subjunctive of be.
words.sentence: The Pevensie children were amazed when they saw talking animals in Narnia.

words.definition: At what time or period.
words.sentence: When the sun rises in Minecraft, the skeletons catch on fire.

words.definition: Belonging to or associated with the person or people that the speaker is addressing.
words.sentence: Remember your Jedi training when you face Darth Vader.

words.definition: Move or travel toward or into a place thought of as near.
words.sentence: Come inside the cozy Hobbit hole and enjoy some warm tea.

words.definition: An unspecified amount or number of.
words.sentence: Bilbo shared some of his finest seed cake with Gandalf.

words.definition: Third person singular present of do.
words.sentence: How does the Sorting Hat know exactly which house to pick?

words.definition: Third person singular present of go.
words.sentence: The Hogwarts Express goes through beautiful green hills on its way to school.

words.definition: Past participle of be.
words.sentence: The One Ring had been lost at the bottom of the river for centuries.

words.definition: A large number of.
words.sentence: There are many dangerous traps hidden inside the ancient pyramid.

tiers.name: Early Academic & Science/Math Words
tiers.description: Learn foundational math and science terms used in elementary learning.
tiers.rule: These words help you describe numbers, nature, and science concepts.
tiers.lessonScript: Even in the farthest outer rim of the galaxy, math and science govern the universe! Whether calculating hyperspace jumps on the Millennium Falcon or brewing complex potions at Hogwarts, mastering academic vocabulary is your key to understanding the cosmos.

sections.name: Math Terms
sections.rule: Use these words to talk about counting, adding, and measuring.
sections.lessonScript: Welcome to Potions class! When you 'ADD' ingredients or calculate the 'SUM' of Smaug's gold, you are using math! Whether measuring Harry's eleven-'INCH' wand or staring at a nine-'FOOT' Enderman, these terms help you measure the world. Remember: 'PLUS' brings things together!

words.definition: Join something to something else so as to increase the size, number, or amount.
words.sentence: In potions class, you must add three crushed beetle eyes to the cauldron.

words.definition: With the addition of.
words.sentence: Two lightsabers plus one blaster equals three weapons.

words.definition: The total amount resulting from the addition of two or more numbers.
words.sentence: The sum of all the gold coins in Smaug's mountain was massive.

words.definition: The relative extent of something; a thing's overall dimensions or magnitude.
words.sentence: The giant spider Aragog was the size of a small house!

words.definition: A unit of linear measure equal to one twelfth of a foot.
words.sentence: Harry's wand was exactly eleven inches long and made of holly.

words.definition: A unit of linear measure equal to twelve inches.
words.sentence: The tall Enderman in Minecraft was over nine feet high.

words.definition: Some but not all of something.
words.sentence: Only part of the secret map was visible under the moonlight.

words.definition: Either of two equal or corresponding parts into which something is or can be divided.
words.sentence: Bilbo gave half of his bread to his dwarf friends.

words.definition: The indefinite continued progress of existence and events.
words.sentence: It was time for the Millennium Falcon to jump to hyperspace.

words.definition: A set of two things used together or regarded as a unit.
words.sentence: Dobby wore a mismatched pair of colorful knitted socks.

sections.name: Science Terms
sections.rule: Use these words to explore nature, earth, and living things.
sections.lessonScript: Step through the wardrobe and feel the cold Narnian 'WIND' and 'RAIN'! Science words describe the natural world, from the solid 'ROCK' of the Lonely Mountain to a Minecraft wheat 'SEED' planted in dark 'DIRT'. Watch your spelling 'GROW' as you explore the living universe!

words.definition: The solid mineral material forming part of the surface of the earth.
words.sentence: The dwarves mined deep into the solid rock of the mountain.

words.definition: The perceptible natural movement of the air.
words.sentence: A cold wind blew across the snowy hills of Narnia.

words.definition: Moisture condensed from the atmosphere that falls visibly in separate drops.
words.sentence: Heavy rain pounded against the windows of the Hogwarts castle.

words.definition: The natural satellite of the earth.
words.sentence: Professor Lupin was very afraid of the full moon.

words.definition: A fixed luminous point in the night sky.
words.sentence: A shooting star streaked across the sky above the Jedi Temple.

words.definition: The condition that distinguishes animals and plants from inorganic matter.
words.sentence: The Force creates and binds all life in the galaxy together.

words.definition: Undergo natural development by increasing in size and changing physically.
words.sentence: Magical plants grow very quickly inside the Hogwarts greenhouses.

words.definition: A flowering plant's unit of reproduction.
words.sentence: Steve planted a wheat seed in the moist dirt near his house.

words.definition: Soil or earth.
words.sentence: The hobbits had rich dark dirt on their bare feet from walking.

words.definition: A woody perennial plant with a single trunk branching out.
words.sentence: The golden leaves of the Lothlórien tree shone beautifully.
